title: Majmua-i-Qissa-i-Shamshad Qissa-i-Khotri, Qissa-i-Larka
gloss: For subject see entry No. 58 at page 17 of the Catalogue for the quarter ending the 31st March 1910.
notes: Cross-reference to 1910Q1 (quarter not yet extracted): entry 58, page 17.

author: Mardan Ali, Munshi
title: Jhok Maula di
notes: Third jhok this quarter (s289 Sikh Gurus, s290 Imam Husain, this one 'of the Lord') — the genre serving all devotional registers.

author: Muhammad Akbar Ali, Maulvi
title: Shir Faroshon ki Imandari
gloss: Verses directed against vendors of impure milk.
notes: 'The Honesty of Milk-Sellers' — satire on adulteration; consumer-morality verse.

author: Muhammad Akbar Ali, Maulvi
title: Qissa-i-Khubsurti-i-zamana
gloss: Verses condemning the manners and customs of the Indians of the present age.
notes: 9th edition — long-running social-critique staple by the same author as s295.

author: Muhammad Akbar Ali, Shaikh
title: Jawab-i-Pagri-Sambhal-o-Jatta
gloss: Verses eulogizing the British rule and condemning the conduct of those who are disaffected.
notes: 'Answer to Pagri Sambhal O Jatta' — loyalist rebuttal of the 1907 agrarian-agitation anthem; direct print-counterinsurgency data point. Same author as s295–296, styled Shaikh here vs Maulvi above.

author: Rahim Bakhsh, Qazi
title: Kaman-i-Dilgudaz, Hissa-i-Awwal
publisher: Author, Daud, tahsil Raya, Siálkot district
notes: 14th edition — highest edition number recorded this quarter; village-author perennial.

flags.issue: reg prints 1331 but duplicates Josh-i-Ghairat (p26 s181, Silcock, whose 1331 is corroborated by the same author holding adjacent reg 1330); this entry may be 1381 (Anand Prakash cluster 1376–1384) — transcribed as printed

author: Sada Ram, Sadhu
title: Qissa-i-Sohni Mahinwal
gloss: The famous story of the love of Sohni and Mahinwal see entry No. 279 on page 13 of this catalogue.
notes: Sammat 1967. Internal cross-reference to s279 (p13, Fazl Shah's 20,000-copy version) — competing Sohni Mahiwal texts in the same quarter.
